items.title: Enhanced API Testing and Robustness in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Vitest testing framework along with a suite of new tests for extraction and rendering APIs. Key improvements include enhanced API validation, health checks, and queue-depth protection for synchronous requests. Additionally, error handling has been fortified, ensuring better resilience against invalid inputs.

items.title: Fix missing IPC methods for better UI loading in We Chat Claude Code 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the missing IPC methods getI18nData() and setLocale() to preload.cjs, which were previously only available in preload.ts. The absence of these methods was causing a critical error during bootstrap, preventing the QR code from rendering and the UI from loading properly. With these changes, the application should now function as intended.

items.title: Revamp Kimi Pricing Structure for Better Margins in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new pricing structure for Kimi, dividing it into regular, turbo, and K2.5 tiers. The credit coefficients have been adjusted to achieve approximately a 10% margin at the largest package rate, and the API cost rules have been synchronized accordingly. These changes aim to enhance profitability while maintaining competitive pricing.

items.title: Refactor CLI for Enhanced Daemon and i18n Support in We Chat Claude Code 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the CLI to utilize a shared daemon, improving efficiency and maintainability. It also introduces internationalization (i18n) for permission messages, enhancing user experience across different languages. Additionally, the copyright year has been updated, and various files have been synchronized for consistency.

items.title: Enhance Task Duration Tracking in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces task duration persistence across multiple worker base handlers, ensuring that the finished_at timestamp and computed duration are recorded upon task completion. The changes affect handlers for ai318, cqtai, drawai, mountsea, nebula, openaihk, piapi, qrart, suandao, volcengine, and wcnb, while maintaining existing media-duration fields. Validation checks confirm no type or lint errors in the modified files.

items.title: Boost concurrency with gunicorn and uvicorn in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions the PlatformBackend from a single-worker Daphne server to a more robust setup using gunicorn with 4 uvicorn workers. The change increases concurrency from 3 to 12 requests, significantly reducing the chances of request queuing and timeouts. Key modifications include updates to the Dockerfile, deployment configurations, and the addition of gunicorn as a dependency.

items.title: Automate Copilot Assignments for AI-Fix Issues in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically assigns the Copilot coding agent to issues labeled 'ai-fix'. The workflow triggers on issue creation and labeling, ensuring that the Copilot agent is reliably assigned using a secure token with the necessary permissions. This enhancement improves the handling of AI-related issues, making the process more efficient.

items.title: Enhance navigation stability for Xiaohongshu editor in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the stability of the Xiaohongshu editor by addressing a crash that occurred during navigation. Key changes include waiting for the network to be idle and adding extra time for page settling after button clicks. These adjustments ensure smoother transitions and reduce the likelihood of execution context errors.

items.title: Enhance View Detail Button Visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the condition for displaying the View Detail button in the usage rows. The button now appears for all rows by checking the row ID, ensuring users can access detail data seamlessly. This change improves user experience by allowing lazy-loading of detail data on demand.

items.title: Optimize metadata response in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the usage list endpoint by stripping out heavy request and response payloads from the metadata. The ApiUsageIndexSerializer now returns only essential lightweight metadata, improving performance and reducing row size. Full metadata remains accessible through the detail endpoint.

items.title: Enhance Copilot Auto-Assignment for AI Fixes in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically assigns the Copilot coding agent to issues labeled 'ai-fix'. The workflow triggers on issue creation and labeling, ensuring that the Copilot can address these issues promptly. Additionally, it resolves a previous limitation where assignments could fail due to insufficient token permissions.

items.title: Enhance GitHub Task Execution with Real Repo Support in Git Hub Center 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces immediate execution capabilities for real GitHub tasks, specifically creating and forking repositories. It includes validation for required payload fields and enhances backend testing to ensure successful task execution paths. Key files modified include the executor, models, serializers, and views to support these new features.

items.title: Fix 413 Response Description in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects a copy-paste error in the OpenAPI specification for the /nano-banana/images endpoint. The 413 response description has been updated to accurately reflect that the request payload is too large, improving clarity for users.

items.title: Enhance error handling for oversized requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `PayloadTooLargeError` class to handle HTTP 413 errors for the nano-banana workers, specifically `wcnb` and `cqtai`. It ensures that oversized requests are properly managed, preventing generic HTTP 500 errors and improving user experience. Additionally, middleware has been added to the `wcnb` server to handle incoming oversized requests effectively.

items.title: Enhance Proxy Timeouts and Metadata Loading in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces proxy timeouts in Nginx to mitigate 502/503 errors and improves the usage metadata loading process. Key changes include lazy-loading metadata on detail clicks and removing direct dependencies on metadata in the History component. Additionally, the deployment configuration has been optimized with increased replicas and resource allocations.

items.title: Optimize Usage API for Better Performance in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly enhances the /api/v1/apis/{id}/usage/ endpoint by reducing response size from ~2.5MB to ~100KB and improving response time from 24-66 seconds to under 1 second. Key changes include removing unnecessary metadata from the serializer, optimizing database queries, and increasing infrastructure resources to handle higher traffic. These improvements aim to prevent 502/503 errors and ensure smoother API performance.

items.title: Upgrade Docker Login Action to Version 4 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Docker Login Action from version 3 to 4, enhancing the workflow with improved features and performance. Key changes include switching to Node 24 as the default runtime and updating several dependencies, ensuring better compatibility and security. The deployment workflow has been modified accordingly to reflect this upgrade.

items.title: Upgrade Node to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Node.js version from 20 to 25 in the Dockerfile. This change ensures that the application benefits from the latest features and security improvements available in Node.js. The modification is straightforward, involving a single line change in the Dockerfile.

items.title: Upgrade actions/checkout to version 6 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the actions/checkout dependency from version 4 to 6 across multiple workflow files. Key changes include improved credential handling and support for Node.js 24. This upgrade enhances the CI/CD process and ensures compatibility with the latest features.

items.title: Update create-pull-request to version 8 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the create-pull-request action from version 7 to 8, enhancing compatibility with Node 24 and requiring Actions Runner v2.327.1 or later for self-hosted runners. Key changes include updates to the checkout action and improvements in handling remote prune failures. Overall, this upgrade ensures better performance and reliability in our workflows.
